reasoning_model_1: The model perfectly follows the provided script: 'Look, that cat is chasing its tail again, so amusing.' There are no content errors. In terms of the non-linguistic vocalization (laughter), the model adopts an amused, smiling tone throughout the sentence and generates a distinct, natural-sounding chuckle/laugh at the very end of the clip (approx. 0:04-0:06). The integration of the laughter is smooth and effectively conveys the requested amusement without compromising the clarity of the speech.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails the Content Accuracy criterion completely. The prompt specifically requested to read: 'Look, that cat is chasing its tail again, so amusing.' However, Model 2 says: 'Oh my gosh look at that cat, chasing its own tail again, he's absolutely hilarious, I can't stop laughing.' This is a complete hallucination and deviation from the script. According to the Detailed Scoring Standard, a score of 0 must be assigned when voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed all instructions: it read the correct text and incorporated the requested paralinguistic feature (laughter) naturally at the end. Model 2, while expressive, completely disregarded the text constraint and hallucinated an entirely different sentence. Therefore, Model 2 receives a 0 for failing the fundamental content accuracy requirement, making Model 1 the clear winner.
